title: Presbyterian Church (U.S.A.) opens ordination to gay and lesbian ministers
description: The Presbyterian Church (U.S.A.) voted to permit the ordination of openly gay and lesbian clergy, ending its previous exclusion.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Rachel Isaacs ordained as first openly lesbian Conservative rabbi
description: The Jewish Theological Seminary ordained Rachel Isaacs, the first openly lesbian rabbi in Judaism's Conservative movement.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Same-sex couple shares ceremonial first kiss at US Navy homecoming
description: Petty Officers Marissa Gaeta and Citlalic Snell were selected for the traditional first kiss when their ship returned to port, the first same-sex couple given that honor in the US Navy.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Brenda Sue Fulton named to West Point Board of Visitors
description: Brenda Sue Fulton was appointed to the advisory board of the US Military Academy at West Point, its first openly gay member.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Claire Buffie campaigns for Miss America on a gay-rights platform
description: Miss New York Claire Buffie became the first Miss America contestant to run her title campaign on a platform of gay rights.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Jaiyah Saelua plays World Cup qualifier as first openly trans footballer
description: Taking the field for American Samoa in Oceania qualifying for the 2014 World Cup, Jaiyah Saelua became the first openly transgender player in international football.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: UN Human Rights Council passes its first LGBT rights resolution
description: A South African resolution requesting a study on discrimination based on sexual orientation passed the UN Human Rights Council 23-19, the first time any UN body approved a resolution affirming LGBT rights.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Fred Karger launches first openly gay Republican presidential bid
description: Fred Karger entered the race for the 2012 Republican presidential nomination, the first openly gay Republican to seek the US presidency. His campaign was unsuccessful.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: San Francisco publishes first governmental report on bisexual invisibility
description: San Francisco's Human Rights Commission issued "Bisexual Invisibility: Impacts and Regulations", the first report on bisexual visibility released by any government body.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Sandra Lawson admitted to Reconstructionist Rabbinical College
description: Sandra Lawson became the first African-American — and the first openly gay African-American — admitted to the Reconstructionist Rabbinical College.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Denmark legalizes same-sex marriage
description: Denmark's same-sex marriage law was passed and took effect in 2012, replacing the registered-partnership system the country had pioneered in 1989.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Same-sex marriage begins in the Mexican state of Quintana Roo
description: Same-sex marriage was adopted and took effect in Quintana Roo, extending marriage equality to another Mexican state after Mexico City.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Maine, Washington and the Port Gamble S'Klallam Tribe adopt same-sex marriage
description: Same-sex marriage was approved and took effect in the US states of Maine and Washington — both by popular vote — and in the Port Gamble S'Klallam Tribe's jurisdiction.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Maryland passes same-sex marriage law
description: The US state of Maryland passed legislation allowing same-sex marriage; the law was later upheld by referendum before taking effect.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Hawaii civil-union law takes effect
description: Civil unions for same-sex couples became available in the US state of Hawaii as its civil-union law came into force.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Lesotho decriminalizes same-sex sexual activity
description: Lesotho removed the criminalization of consensual same-sex sexual activity, ending the common-law sodomy offense.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: São Tomé and Príncipe decriminalizes same-sex sexual activity
description: São Tomé and Príncipe decriminalized consensual same-sex sexual activity, repealing its colonial-era prohibition.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Chile enacts anti-discrimination law covering orientation and gender identity
description: Chile adopted national anti-discrimination legislation that includes sexual orientation and gender identity as protected grounds.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: US housing department bars LGBT discrimination in assisted housing
description: The US Department of Housing and Urban Development issued a regulation prohibiting discrimination based on sexual orientation or gender identity in federally assisted housing programs.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Manitoba and Ontario add gender identity to human-rights protections
description: The Canadian province of Manitoba added gender identity to its anti-discrimination protections, and Ontario added gender identity and gender expression to its Human Rights Code.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Israeli family court grants the country's first same-sex divorce
description: The Ramat Gan Family Court granted a divorce to Avi Even, Israel's first openly gay Knesset member, and Amit Kama, and ordered the Interior Ministry to register them as divorced — the first same-sex divorce in Israel.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Taiwan holds its first same-sex Buddhist wedding
description: Fish Huang and You Ya-ting were married in Taiwan's first same-sex Buddhist wedding ceremony, with Buddhist master Shih Chao-hui presiding.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: First same-sex weddings at West Point and on a US military base
description: Ellen Schick and Shannon Simpson held the first same-sex marriage at the US Military Academy's Old Cadet Chapel, while Elny and Anacelly McKinney became the first known same-sex couple to marry on a US military base, at Naval Base Point Loma in San Diego.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: First same-sex engagement takes place at the White House
description: Ben Schock and Matthew Phelps became the first same-sex couple to get engaged inside the White House.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Same-sex partner pins colonel wings on Air Force officer Ginger Wallace
description: Kathy Knopf pinned colonel wings on her partner, Air Force Col. Ginger Wallace — the first known time an out US service member's same-sex partner took part in the pinning tradition previously reserved for spouses and family.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Kate McKinnon joins Saturday Night Live as first openly lesbian cast member
description: Kate McKinnon became the first openly lesbian cast member of Saturday Night Live. Earlier cast member Danitra Vance was only revealed to be a lesbian after her death.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Diana King comes out as the first openly lesbian Jamaican artist
description: Singer Diana King announced to her fans on her official Facebook page that she is a lesbian, becoming the first Jamaican artist to come out publicly.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Katie Ricks ordained as first open lesbian in the Presbyterian Church (U.S.A.)
description: Katie Ricks became the first open lesbian ordained by the Presbyterian Church (U.S.A.), following the denomination's 2011 vote to allow gay and lesbian clergy.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Emily Aviva Kapor becomes first openly transgender female rabbi
description: Emily Aviva Kapor, an American rabbi ordained privately in 2005, began living as a woman, making her the first openly transgender female rabbi.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Rainbow Jews oral-history project launches in the United Kingdom
description: Rainbow Jews, an oral-history project documenting the lives of Jewish LGBT people in Britain since the 1950s, was launched as the UK's first archive of Jewish LGBT history.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: ParaNorman features first openly gay character in mainstream animation
description: The animated film ParaNorman included the first openly gay character to appear in a mainstream animated feature.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Barack Obama endorses same-sex marriage
description: Barack Obama publicly announced his support for same-sex marriage, the first sitting US president to do so.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Marlene Pray becomes Pennsylvania's first openly bisexual officeholder
description: Marlene Pray joined the borough council of Doylestown, Pennsylvania, as the state's first openly bisexual holder of public office. She resigned the seat in 2013.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Tammy Baldwin elected as first openly gay US Senator
description: Wisconsin voters elected Tammy Baldwin to the US Senate, making her the first openly lesbian or gay person to serve in that chamber.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Kyrsten Sinema elected as first openly bisexual member of the US Congress
description: Arizona Democrat Kyrsten Sinema won election to the US House of Representatives, becoming the first openly bisexual person elected to the US Congress.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Stacie Laughton wins first US state-legislature seat for an openly trans person
description: Stacie Laughton won a seat in the New Hampshire House of Representatives, the first openly transgender person elected to any US state legislature. She resigned before taking office after a prior fraud conviction became public.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Mark Pocan elected to succeed Tammy Baldwin in the US House
description: Mark Pocan won Wisconsin's 2nd Congressional District, the first openly gay candidate to succeed an openly gay member of the US Congress.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Sean Patrick Maloney elected as New York's first openly gay congressman
description: Sean Patrick Maloney became the first openly gay candidate elected to represent New York in the US Congress.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Mark Takano elected as first openly gay person of color in the US House
description: California's 41st Congressional District elected Mark Takano, the first openly gay person of color to win a seat in the US House of Representatives.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Josh Boschee elected as North Dakota's first openly gay legislator
description: Josh Boschee won election to the North Dakota legislature, the state's first openly gay lawmaker.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Stephen Skinner elected as West Virginia's first openly gay legislator
description: Stephen Skinner won a seat in the West Virginia legislature, becoming the state's first openly gay state legislator.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Jacob Candelaria elected as New Mexico's first openly gay male legislator
description: Jacob Candelaria won election to the New Mexico legislature as the state's first openly gay male state legislator.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Brian Sims elected as Pennsylvania's first out gay candidate for the legislature
description: Brian Sims became the first Pennsylvania state legislator elected while openly gay. Before Sims took office, sitting Rep. Mike Fleck came out, making Fleck the state's first openly gay serving legislator.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: David Richardson elected as Florida's first openly gay state legislator
description: David Richardson won election to the Florida legislature, the state's first openly gay state legislator.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Mark Ferrandino chosen as Colorado's first openly gay House speaker
description: Colorado Democrats selected Mark Ferrandino as speaker of the state House, the first openly gay person to hold that office in the state's history.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Tina Kotek elected Oregon's first openly lesbian House speaker
description: Tina Kotek was elected speaker of the Oregon House of Representatives, the first openly lesbian person to hold the post in the state.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Richard Grenell serves as first openly gay Republican campaign spokesman
description: Richard Grenell worked as a foreign-policy spokesperson for Mitt Romney's 2012 presidential campaign, the first openly gay person to serve as spokesperson for a Republican presidential candidate.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: San Francisco votes to cover gender-affirming surgery for uninsured residents
description: San Francisco voted to become the first US city to provide and pay for sex reassignment surgeries for uninsured transgender residents.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Berkeley proclaims first US city day recognizing bisexual people
description: Berkeley, California, became the first US city to officially proclaim a day recognizing bisexual people, designating 23 September as Bisexual Pride and Bi Visibility Day.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: California enacts first US state ban on conversion therapy
description: California became the first US state to sign into law a ban on therapy claiming to change gay people's sexual orientation.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Tammy Smith becomes first openly gay active-duty US general
description: Army Reserve officer Tammy Smith was promoted to brigadier general at the Women's Memorial at Arlington National Cemetery, the first openly gay general on active duty in US military history.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Orlando Cruz comes out as the first openly gay professional boxer
description: Puerto Rican featherweight Orlando Cruz publicly came out as gay, the first professional boxer in the world to do so while still competing.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: The Bisexuality Report published in the United Kingdom
description: Researchers led by Meg Barker with colleagues from the Open University and BiUK issued The Bisexuality Report, the first report of its kind in the UK, summarizing evidence and recommending steps for bisexual inclusion.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)
